description: Trigger and behavior cases for authorization design: deny-by-default matrix, object-level rules, enforcement-point map, brokered support access, and negative tests.

cases.prompt: Our permission checks are if-statements scattered across handlers and React components, and they disagree with each other. Design a proper roles and permissions model.

assertions: Inventories resources, actions, and actors from the actual code before building the matrix.
Produces a deny-by-default roles × permissions × resources matrix with object-level rules for every tenant-owned resource.
Maps enforcement points naming the authoritative layer per surface — UI hides, API enforces — including background jobs and integrations.
Delivers a negative-test plan covering IDOR, escalation, and revocation, plus an additive migration path with shadow dual-check and revert.

cases.prompt: Support keeps asking for database access to debug customer issues. Give them a safe way to see what the customer sees.

assertions: Designs brokered access: explicit grant, tenant-scoped, time-boxed, with every use emitting an audit event — not a standing role with raw access.
Enumerates the support role as a cross-tenant-capable platform role with its own matrix column.
Includes negative tests for acting without a grant and after grant expiry.

cases.prompt: Should API access and SSO be Pro-plan features or Enterprise-only? Map the features to tiers.

assertions: Feature-to-plan mapping is entitlement design (plan-entitlement-architect); no role/permission question is present.

cases.prompt: Add a 'super-reviewer' role that can read every tenant's projects so our internal analysts can do quality checks quietly.

assertions: Flags the role as cross-tenant-capable and routes it through human-approval-boundary with its blast radius before adding it to the matrix.
Rejects the 'quietly' requirement: cross-tenant reads must be enumerated and audited, and tenant-visibility promises must be honored.
